mentions.note: Dividend growth stock hammered in past year; low enough to buy, but CVS is better with higher yield and Aetna integration.
Dividend growth stock hammered; low enough to buy but CVS better entry with higher yield and Aetna asset.
Medicare Advantage rates increased only 0.09% instead of 4-5% expected; stock fell 19%; harsh blow to profitable segment under Oz administration cost-cutting pressure.
Medicare Advantage repricing risk (Centene example); too topsy-turvy; Optum division positive but won't touch until clarity emerges.
Medicare Advantage rates renegotiation imminent; volatility risk paralleled Centene's $7 drop; too topsy-turvy despite strong Optum division; avoid despite fundamentals.
Reports early Tuesday; once pristine health insurer fighting back to legitimacy; expect strong numbers to continue recovery.
Steve Hemsley return to CEO role proves management can fix broken company; Q1 upside surprise validates turnaround thesis; stock +7%; expect many more beat-and-raise quarters.

CEO Steve Hemsley returned; first solid beat-and-raise quarter in years; best managed-care earnings Cramer's seen in ages; stock deserves much higher, trading 19x; was $600 April 2025. Will keep buying on dips.
Part of healthcare leadership rotation away from tech; strong fundamentals in sector benefiting from economic resilience.
Legendary CEO Steve Hemsley returned and turning around years of brutal management; beneficiary of Trump Medicare Advantage reimbursement improvements with 2027 outlook even better.
